claim: Brady's Syriac EVA-to-consonant character map, applied to Hand A folios IN ISOLATION (no Hand B tokens mixed in), produces decoded text whose in-order word-order coherence exceeds the shuffled-corpus baseline on the connector-to-content bigram metric. If so, the original cross-corpus Brady-shuffle-test failure (Brain-V 2026-04-15) was due to Hand B masking a real Hand-A signal.
evidence_for: H-BV-HAND-A-INTERNAL-01 just confirmed Hand A is natural-language-like
Brady's original methodology mixed both hands; if Hand B is a different system, its shuffle-test noise could have drowned out Hand-A signal
Brady's mapping (kaph/qoph merger, pharyngeal drop, q->waw) was derived from statistical properties that may be concentrated in Hand A
evidence_against: Four consecutive cross-hand-semantics refutations already showed Hand A and Hand B don't share linguistic material, so the 'masking' hypothesis has a strong prior against it

Brain-V's earlier shuffle test on Brady's proxy lexicon produced connector-content bigram delta of -0.0098 (in-order WORSE than shuffled) across the whole corpus; a simple hand-restriction may not flip the sign
Hand A shares consonant-skeleton vocabulary with itself more than with Hand B, so applying Brady's map to Hand A gives only Hand-A output — but that output must still show syntactic structure to pass
test:
Apply Brady's EVA-to-Syriac-consonant character map (identical to earlier volvelle and Brady tests) to every token on every Hand-A folio. Produce line-level sequences of Syriac consonant skeletons. Apply Brain-V's Syriac proxy lexicon (71 skeletons from earlier Brady test) to label each skeleton as MATCHED/UNMATCHED and classify CONNECTOR ({w, d, l, m, k, kl, km, dy, ky}) or CONTENT.

Compute shuffle-test metrics on the Hand-A-only decoded stream:
  in-order connector-content bigram rate (fraction of connector tokens followed by non-connector content)
  shuffled connector-content bigram rate (same token pool shuffled across corpus)
  delta = in-order - shuffled

Compare to original full-corpus Brady test: delta was -0.0098 (in-order worse).
test_threshold: CONFIRMED if Hand-A-only connector-content bigram delta is POSITIVE and >= +0.010. MARGINAL if 0 < delta < +0.010. REFUTED if delta <= 0 (Hand A alone also fails the shuffle test, consistent with earlier cross-corpus result).

tests_run.details: MARGINAL +0.0044 (threshold +0.010). Sign FLIPPED from full-corpus -0.0098 to positive within Hand A alone — Hand B noise was partially masking a Hand-A signal. Magnitude below confirmed threshold but directionally supportive of Brady within Hand A.

reasoning: Hand A passed the language-structure internal test (H-BV-HAND-A-INTERNAL-01 confirmed). The natural next check: does any existing character-map hypothesis (Brady's is the best-worked-out option) survive when restricted to Hand A? If yes, Brady's Syriac hypothesis gets new legs under the restriction; if no, Hand A has linguistic structure but Brady's specific map is not the right key.
